task_completion.description: Open the three required input images in Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji interface showing image windows for 'donor_channel.tif', 'acceptor_channel.tif', and 'fret_raw_channel.tif'.

task_completion.description: Ensure calculations are performed with 32-bit float precision
task_completion.visual_evidence: Image windows showing '32-bit' in their title/status bar, OR the '32-bit (float) result' checkbox ticked in the Image Calculator dialog.

task_completion.description: Apply the FRET correction formula subtracting donor and acceptor bleed-through
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji 'Image Calculator' dialogs showing subtraction/multiplication operations, 'Math > Macro' dialog, or a Fiji script editor showing the formula with coefficients 0.28 (for Donor) and 0.19 (for Acceptor).

task_completion.description: Save the resulting corrected image to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the directory '~/Fiji_Data/results/fret/' and the filename 'corrected_fret.tif', or a terminal/file manager showing the file successfully created in that exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji (ImageJ) application to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Fiji GUI windows, toolbars, and dialogs are visible in the screenshots, rather than the agent exclusively using terminal commands or external Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the math operations
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of Image Calculator dialogs, Math dialogs, or a Fiji macro being executed, rather than just renaming or moving an existing file.

integrity.description: The output is a genuine image with the expected visual features
integrity.visual_evidence: The final 'corrected_fret.tif' image window shows actual image data (blobs) and is not a blank canvas or a text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correction formula uses coefficients 0.28 for the donor channel and 0.19 for the acceptor channel. The input images contain synthetic blobs. A successful correction will result in an image where the donor-only region (top left, center 125,125) and acceptor-only region (top right, center 387,125) have pixel values close to 0 (bleed-through eliminated), while the FRET interaction region (bottom center, center 256,387) retains a high signal (~100).
